title: sambucus canadensis subsp. laciniata
intro: Sambucus canadensis subsp. laciniata, also known as cutleaf black elder or cutleaf elderberry, is a subspecies of the Sambucus canadensis plant, which is a flowering plant native to eastern North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating colds, fever, and other respiratory issues, due to its antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Sambucus canadensis subsp. laciniata include reducing the severity and duration of cold and flu symptoms, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. Sambucus canadensis subsp. laciniata is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, to promote overall health and well-being.
